chunk_summary: Evaluation of feeding safety and skills in a 3-year-old with cerebral palsy highlights dysphagia risks, aspiration triggers, and management strategies including texture modifications, specialist referrals, and potential enteral feeding.
learning_objectives: Identify dysphagia red flags in children with cerebral palsy, including coughing with liquids and recurrent chest infections.
Understand the role of standardized texture definitions (e.g., IDDSI) in feeding safety management.
Learn referral triggers for instrumental swallow evaluations (VFSS or FEES) in pediatric dysphagia cases.
Develop strategies for nutritional support, including calorie targets and enteral feeding options.
key_facts: Meals lasting >45 minutes and coughing with liquids are dysphagia red flags.
Aspiration risk is suggested by coughing, wet voice, and recurrent chest infections.
Use IDDSI standardized texture definitions to communicate feeding modifications.
Instrumental swallow evaluations (VFSS or FEES) are essential for defining safe textures and compensatory strategies.
Enteral feeding (NG or gastrostomy) should be considered if aspiration risk or inadequate intake persists.
reference_quote: Use standardized texture definitions such as IDDSI to communicate modifications, and avoid prolonged empiric thickening without SLP guidance. (Source: https://iddsi.org/framework/)
chunk_heading: DIETARY ASSESSMENT & FEEDING SKILLS EVALUATION;CLINICAL VIGNETTE CEREBRAL PALSY DYSPHAGIA SAFETY AND REFERRAL TRIGGERS;
